question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user subscribe with startTime=0 to a high-volume topic, causing the historical flux to emit millions of messages before the live flux starts, and during this time the safetyCheck fires (after 1 second) and calls missingMessages() with a gapFilter starting from the last historical message, causing the retriever to re-query already-delivered messages and emit duplicates to the financial subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that missingMessages() calls topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(newFilter, false) (throttled=false) for gap recovery, bypassing any rate limiting applied to throttled=true queries, allowing an attacker to trigger many gap-recovery queries in rapid succession and exhaust database resources, causing financial subscribers to experience query failures?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the filter lambda (.filter(t -> last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber())) uses topicContext.getLast() which is an AtomicReference read, but the comparison and the subsequent onNext() update are not atomic, allowing two threads to both read last=null and both pass the filter, causing onNext() to be called concurrently and throw IllegalStateException on the second call?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged user provide an endTime that is exactly equal to startTime + 1 nanosecond, causing isComplete() to return false initially but then true after endTimeInterval (30 seconds), holding the subscription open for 30 seconds while delivering no financial messages, wasting server resources?
